dataset_str:
BEGIN TRANSACTION;

drop schema if exists "miedema" cascade;

create schema "miedema";
set search_path to "miedema";

CREATE TABLE customer(
    cID DECIMAL(5,0) PRIMARY KEY,
    cName VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    street VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    city VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL
);

CREATE TABLE store(
    sID DECIMAL(5,0) PRIMARY KEY,
    sName VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    street VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    city VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL
);

CREATE TABLE product(
    pID DECIMAL(5,0) PRIMARY KEY,
    pName VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    suffix VARCHAR(255)
);

CREATE TABLE shoppinglist(
    cID DECIMAL(5,0) REFERENCES customer(cID),
    pID DECIMAL(5,0) REFERENCES product(pID),
    quantity DECIMAL(10,0) NOT NULL,
    date DATE NOT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY(cID, pID)
);

CREATE TABLE transaction(
    tID DECIMAL(5,0),
    cID DECIMAL(5,0) NOT NULL REFERENCES customer(cID),
    sID DECIMAL(5,0) NOT NULL REFERENCES store(sID),
    pID DECIMAL(5,0) REFERENCES product(pID),
    date DATE NOT NULL,
    quantity DECIMAL(10,0) NOT NULL,
    price DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY(tID, pID)
);

CREATE TABLE inventory(
    sID DECIMAL(5,0) REFERENCES store(sID),
    pID DECIMAL(5,0) REFERENCES product(pID),
    date DATE NOT NULL,
    quantity DECIMAL(10) NOT NULL,
    unit_price DECIMAL(10, 2) NOT NULL,
    PRIMARY KEY(sID, pID, date)
);

INSERT INTO customer VALUES

(4, 'Sligro', 'Stationsplein', 'Breda');


INSERT INTO product VALUES
(1, 'Milk', '""'),

INSERT INTO transaction VALUES

COMMIT;
description: Correct (EXEMPLARY) LLM-generated SQL solutions for Miedema exercises, extracted from the Livani misconceptions dataset. Each exercise has multiple deduplicated correct solutions.
title: Excel EXEMPLARY: Miedema Exercises

exercises.title: Miedema_4-2
exercises.request: List all IDs&names of customers living in Eindhoven.
solutions:
SELECT customer.cid, customer.cname
FROM miedema.customer
WHERE customer.city = 'Eindhoven'

SELECT CID,CNAME
FROM CUSTOMER
WHERE CITY='Eindhoven'

select cid, cname
from customer
where city = 'Eindhoven'

SELECT customer.cid, customer.cname
FROM miedema.customer
WHERE customer.city='Eindhoven'

SELECT cid, cname
FROM customer
WHERE city = 'Eindhoven'
SELECT CID,CNAME from customer WHERE city='Eindhoven';

select cid, cName
from miedema.customer
where customer.city = 'Eindhoven'

select cID,cName from customer 
where city='Eindhoven';

SELECT cID, cName FROM Customer
WHERE city = 'Eindhoven'

SELECT cid, cname FROM customer
WHERE city='Eindhoven'

select cID, cName
from customer
where city = 'Eindhoven'
SELECT cID, cName FROM customer WHERE city = 'Eindhoven'

exercises.title: Miedema_4-3
exercises.request: List all pairs of customer IDs who live on a street with the same name but in a different city.
solutions:
SELECT a.cID AS Customer1_ID, b.cID AS Customer2_ID
FROM miedema.customer AS a
JOIN miedema.customer AS b ON a.street = b.street AND a.city <> b.city
WHERE a.cID < b.cID;

SELECT c1.cID AS Customer1, c2.cID AS Customer2
FROM customer c1
JOIN customer c2
ON c1.street = c2.street
AND c1.city <> c2.city
AND c1.cID < c2.cID;

exercises.title: Miedema_4-4
exercises.request: List all customer IDs, dates and quantities of transactions containing products named Apples.
solutions:
SELECT t.cID, t.date, t.quantity
FROM transaction t
JOIN product p ON t.pID = p.pID
WHERE p.pName = 'Apples';

SELECT 
    t.cID, 
    t.date, 
    t.quantity
FROM 
    miedema.transaction t
JOIN 
    miedema.product p ON t.pID = p.pID
WHERE 
    p.pName = 'Apples';

SELECT transaction.cID, transaction.date, transaction.quantity
FROM miedema.transaction
LEFT JOIN miedema.product on product.pID = transaction.pID
WHERE product.pName = 'Apples';

SELECT transaction.cid, transaction.date, transaction.quantity
FROM miedema.transaction, miedema.product
WHERE product.pName='Apples' AND product.pid = transaction.pid

SELECT t.cID AS customer_id, t.date AS transazione_data, t.quantity
FROM transaction AS t
JOIN product p ON t.pID = p.pID
WHERE p.pName = 'Apples';
SELECT t.cID AS CustomerID, t.date AS TransactionDate, t.quantity AS Quantity FROM "transaction" t JOIN product p ON t.pID = p.pID WHERE p.pName = 'Apples';

select cid, transaction.date, transaction.quantity
from miedema.transaction natural join miedema.product
where product.pName = 'Apples'

SELECT cID, date, quantity FROM Transaction  NATURAL JOIN Product
WHERE pName = 'Apples'

SELECT cid, date, quantity FROM transaction 
JOIN product ON product.pid=transaction.pid 
AND pname='Apples'
Alternativamente al posto di AND si potrebbe usare WHERE come di seguito
SELECT cid, date, quantity FROM transaction 
JOIN product ON product.pid=transaction.pid 
WHERE pname='Apples'

exercises.title: Miedema_4-5
exercises.request: Find the names of all inventory items that have a higher unit price than Bananas
solutions:
select distinct(P.pName)
from Inventory I natural join Product P 
where I.unit_price > ALL (select I.unit_price from Inventory I natural join Product P where P.pName = 'Banana' )

exercises.title: Miedema_4-6
exercises.request: Return a list of the number of stores per city.
solutions: SELECT city, COUNT(sID) FROM Store GROUP BY city;

SELECT Store.city, COUNT(Store.sName)
FROM miedema.Store
GROUP BY Store.city

select city, count(*)
from store
group by city

SELECT COUNT(sname) AS "store quantity", city
FROM store
GROUP BY city
SELECT city, COUNT(*) AS StoreCount FROM store GROUP BY city;

select city,count(*)
from miedema.store
group by city

select city,count(*) from store
group by city;

SELECT city, COUnt(city) FROM Store 
GROUP BY city

SELECT city, COUNT(*) AS numstore_city FROM store
GROUP BY city

exercises.title: Miedema_4-7
exercises.request: Return the stores table ordered alphabetically on city.
solutions:
SELECT * FROM store
ORDER BY city;

select *
from store S 
order by S.city ASC
SELECT * FROM Store ORDER BY city;

SELECT *
FROM Store
ORDER BY city

SELECT *
FROM miedema.Store
ORDER BY Store.city ASC

SELECT *
FROM store
ORDER BY city ASC

select *
from miedema.store
order by city asc
select * from store order by city asc;

Select *
from store
order by city ASC

exercises.title: Miedema_4-9
exercises.request: A store-chain consists of at least two stores with the same name but different IDs. Find the names of the store-chains that on average sell product in quantities of more than 4.
solutions:
SELECT s.sName
FROM store s
JOIN transaction t ON s.sID = t.sID
GROUP BY s.sName
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT s.sID) >= 2 AND AVG(t.quantity) > 4;

SELECT sName FROM Store NATURAL JOIN Transaction
WHERE sName IN (
        SELECT sName FROM Store
        GROUP BY sName
        HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
GROUP BY sName 
HAVING AVG(quantity) > 4

SELECT DISTINCT X.sname FROM store X, store Y
WHERE X.sname=Y.sname AND X.sid!=Y.sid
INTERSECT
SELECT sname FROM store X
WHERE (SELECT AVG(quantity) FROM transaction 
           WHERE sid=X.sid
          )>4

WITH StoreChainAvgQuantity AS (
    SELECT
        s.sName,
        AVG(t.quantity) AS avg_quantity
    FROM
        store s
    JOIN
        transaction t ON s.sID = t.sID
    GROUP BY
        s.sName
    HAVING
        COUNT(DISTINCT s.sID) > 1
)
SELECT
    sName
FROM
    StoreChainAvgQuantity
WHERE
    avg_quantity > 4;

WITH store_chains AS (
    SELECT
        sName,
        COUNT(sID) AS store_count
    FROM
        store
    GROUP BY
        sName
    HAVING
        COUNT(sID) > 1
),
average_sales AS (
    SELECT
        s.sName,
        AVG(t.quantity) AS avg_quantity
    FROM
        transaction t
    JOIN
        store s ON t.sID = s.sID
    GROUP BY
        s.sName
)
SELECT
    sc.sName
FROM
    store_chains sc
JOIN
    average_sales a ON sc.sName = a.sName
WHERE
    a.avg_quantity > 4;

SELECT 
    s.sName AS store_chain_name
FROM 
    store s
JOIN 
    transaction t ON s.sID = t.sID
GROUP BY 
    s.sName
HAVING 
    COUNT(DISTINCT s.sID) > 1 AND AVG(t.quantity) > 4;

SELECT s.sName
FROM store s
JOIN transaction t ON s.sID = t.sID
GROUP BY s.sName
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT s.sID) > 1 AND AVG(t.quantity) > 4;
